codeReview.summary: The rerun fix now threads EffectivePolicy.ProviderRoute into runtime provider resolution through ProviderRegistry/WithRegistry, while preserving fallback behavior and existing GitLab/LLM throttling and Redis degraded-mode wiring. I did not find a remaining code issue in the reviewed scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-reliability skill does not explicitly call out end-to-end runtime wiring checks when a reliability feature also changes integration selection paths, which likely contributed to the original worker shipping only unit-level ProviderRoute metadata checks before the rerun added real runtime coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Original feature transcript/handoff says procedure was followed, but the original added test at internal/llm/provider_test.go only checked rules metadata; the rerun added runtime-level tests such as TestProviderRoutePolicySelectsRuntimeProvider and TestProviderRouteEndToEnd in internal/llm/provider_test.go that actually exercise Processor.WithRegistry and ProcessRun route selection.

summary: Review passed on rerun: the fix replaces hard-coded worker route selection with policy-driven runtime resolution via ProviderRegistry, and the added runtime tests demonstrate that policy-selected providers, fallback routing, and existing throttling/degraded behavior still work together.
